name: HMCS Giffard (K 402)
events: 7 May 1944 HMCS Giffard (T/A/Lt.Cdr. C. Petersen, RCNR) picks up 43 survivors from HMCS Valleyfield that was torpedoed and sunk about 50 miles south of Cape Race, Newfoundland in position 46°03'N, 52°24'W by German U-boat U-548. 5 of the survivors later died of exposure.

28 Aug 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) conducted A/S exercises off St.Johns with ships of Escort Group C 1, the frigate HMCS New Glasgow (T/Cdr. G.S. Hall, RCNR) and the corvettes HMCS Arnprior (T/Lt. S.D. Thom, DSC, RCNVR), HMCS Frontenac (T/Lt. M.C. Bull, RCNVR), HMCS Giffard (T/Lt. G.H. Matheson, RCNR) and HMCS Fredericton (T/Lt. J.C. Smyth, RCNR). It is possible the frigate HMCS Joliette (T/A/Lt.Cdr. G.N. Downey, RCNR) and / or the corvette HMCS Chambly (T/Lt. S.D. Taylor, RCNR) also participated. (1)
28 Mar 1945 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Bermuda with HMCS Giffard (T/Lt. G.H. Matheson, RCNR) and HMCS Morden (T/Lt. F.R. Spidler, RCNVR). (2)
29 Mar 1945 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Bermuda with HMCS Giffard (T/Lt. G.H. Matheson, RCNR) and HMCS Morden (T/Lt. F.R. Spidler, RCNVR). (2)
